children.heading: Land and Water Conservation Fund Act
children.label: a Land and Water Conservation Fund Act
children.text: Subsections (a), (b), (c), (d), (e), (f), (g), and (i) (except for paragraph (1)(C)) of section 4 of the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act of 1965 are repealed, except that the Secretary may continue to issue Golden Eagle Passports, Golden Age Passports, and Golden Access Passports under such section until the date the notice required by is published in the Federal Register regarding the establishment of the National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass.1section 6804(a)(3) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Recreational fee demonstration program
children.label: b Recreational fee demonstration program
children.text: Section 315 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1996 (as contained in ), is repealed.1section 101(c) of Public Law 104–134

children.heading: Admission permits for refuge units
children.label: c Admission permits for refuge units

children.heading: National park passport, Golden Eagle Passport, Golden Age Passport, and Golden Access Passport
children.label: d National park passport, Golden Eagle Passport, Golden Age Passport, and Golden Access Passport

children.text: Section 502 of the National Parks Omnibus Management Act of 1998 ().1Public Law 105–391
Title VI of the National Parks Omnibus Management Act of 1998 ().1Public Law 105–391

children.heading: Land and water conservation fund special accounts
children.label: 1 Land and water conservation fund special accounts
children.text: Amounts in the special accounts established under section 4(i)(1) of the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act of 1965 for Federal land management agencies that are unobligated on , shall be transferred to the appropriate special account established under and shall be available to the Secretary in accordance with this chapter. A special account established under section 4(i)(1) of the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act of 1965 for a Federal agency that is not a Federal land management area, and the use of such special account, is not affected by the repeal of section 4 of the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act of 1965 by subsection (a) of this section.111December 8, 2004section 6806 of this title

children.heading: National parks passport
children.label: 2 National parks passport
children.text: Any funds collected under title VI of the National Parks Omnibus Management Act of 1998 () that are unobligated on the day before the publication of the Federal Register notice required under shall be transferred to the special account of the National Park Service for use in accordance with this chapter. The Secretary of the Interior may use amounts available in that special account to pay any outstanding administration, marketing, or close-out costs associated with the national parks passport.1Public Law 105–391section 6804(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Recreational fee demonstration program
children.label: 3 Recreational fee demonstration program
children.text: Any funds collected in accordance with section 315 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1996 (as contained in ), that are unobligated on the day before , shall be transferred to the appropriate special account and shall be available to the Secretary in accordance with this chapter.1section 101(c) of Public Law 104–134December 8, 2004

children.heading: Admission permits for refuge units
children.label: 4 Admission permits for refuge units
children.text: Any funds collected in accordance with that are available as provided in subsection (c)(A) of such section and are unobligated on the day before , shall be transferred to the special account of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service for use in accordance with this chapter.section 3911 of this titleDecember 8, 2004

children.heading: Effect of regulations
children.label: f Effect of regulations
children.text: A regulation or policy issued under a provision of law repealed by this section shall remain in effect to the extent such a regulation or policy is consistent with the provisions of this chapter until the Secretary issues a regulation, guideline, or policy under this chapter that supersedes the earlier regulation.
